title: John Jackson Miller
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
{{Otheruses|John}}
{{Person
|image=[[File:JohnJacksonMiller.jpg]]
|name=John Jackson Miller
|birth=[[January 11]], [[1968]]<ref name="Comics Industry Birthdays">{{WebCite|url=https://cbgxtra.com/knowledge-base/for-your-reference/comics-industry-birthdays|text=John Jackson Miller: F.A.O.|author=Miller, John|archivedate=20110218031356}}</ref>
|death=
|listedpronouns=<!--Only include pronouns if they have been explicitly specified by the individual to which this article pertains.-->
|nationality=[[Wikipedia:United States|American]]
|occupation=[[Wikipedia:Writer|Writer]]
|companies=*[[Dark Horse Comics]]
*[[Del Rey]]
|website=[https://www.farawaypress.com farawaypress.com]

'''John Jackson Miller''' is an American comic-book writer, novelist and commentator, known for the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] and [[Star Wars: Knight Errant|''Knight Errant'']] comic series published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. He also wrote the short story ''[[Labor Pains]]'', the novel [[Kenobi (novel)|''Kenobi'']], and co-wrote the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'', among many others. Many of Jackson's stories explore the responsibilities held by [[Jedi]].<ref name="Miller themes">{{Twitter|jjmfaraway|status/1699833698122887384|[[John Jackson Miller]]|quote=My first Star Wars novel since A NEW DAWN, THE LIVING FORCE elaborates on themes about Jedi responsibility I explored in that work, as well as KNIGHTS OF THE OLD REPUBLIC, KNIGHT ERRANT, LOST TRIBE OF THE SITH, and KENOBI. Plus plenty of action! |archivedate=20231024181426}}</ref>

He has edited magazines including ''Comics & Games Retailer'', ''Comics Buyer's Guide'', and ''[[SCRYE Magazine|Scrye: The Guide to Collectible Card Games]]'', and written books including ''The Standard Catalog of Comic Books''. He also researches comic-book circulation figures on his history site ''[http://www.comichron.com The Comics Chronicles]''. His first professional comics work appeared in [[2003]] in [[Wikipedia:Crimson Dynamo|''Crimson Dynamo'']] for [[Marvel Comics]], which led to a run on [[Wikipedia:Iron Man|''Iron Man'']]. His first ''Star Wars'' work appeared in [[2005]] in [[Empire 35|''Star Wars: Empire'']].

Miller also wrote the comic adaptation of ''Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull'' in [[2009]].<ref name="Jones">{{Faraway|url=Home/tabid/58/EntryID/106/Default.aspx|text=If adventure has a name...}}</ref>

In [[2015]], Miller released his first ''Star Trek'' novel, ''Star Trek: The Next Generation: Takedown'', to greatly positive reader acclaim.<ref name="takedown">{{Amazon|url=Star-Trek-Next-Generation-Takedown/dp/1476782717|text=Star Trek: The Next Generation: Takedown}}</ref>

His most recent book was ''Batman: Resurrection,'' an original novel set in between the [[1989]] live action film adaptation of ''Batman'' and its [[1992]] sequel ''Batman Returns,'' released on October 15, [[2024]].
